caption: A great book with lessons on how to find balance, main key takeaways: Finding balance is about patience and maintenance, practicing day by day. Lessons of the balance are: 1.avoidance of pain leads to more pain, 2. Recovery begins with abstinence, 3.abstinence resets the brain pathway and let us find joy in simpler pleasures, 4. Self binding practices to put physical and cognitive space between desire and consumption 5. There is a loss when we medicate our way through the pain 6. Pressing on the pain can reset our balance for the pleasure 7. Be aware of getting addicted to the pain 8. Radical honesty is important to promote awareness, enhances intimacy and fosters growth, 9. Pro social shame makes us belong to the human tribe, leverage shame to help you stay in balance 10. Find escape from pain by immersing yourself in it #bookrecommendations

Fun and informative read! Key takeaways: Possibilities are adjacent, networks liquid, hunches slow, errors are part of the game, serendipity plays a role, and repurposing tools is natural. We can also help promote innovation platforms by building infrastructure that allows open discussions, definitely time to start on that idea journaling and having more conversations with people!  #bookrecommendations

Fun read, key takeaways were: embrace being wrong, enjoy learning and challenging assumptions and methodology, identify by your core values not your opinions, active listening and effective communication are essential tools for getting anything productive done, have humility, curiosity and a scientific mindset in all aspects of life, and build your own challenge network to help you grow! #bookrecommendations

Here’s a collection of my recent listens along with some key takeaways that I enjoyed:
1. Absolutely fun and a great reminder of why I love science! The scientific methods provide a rigorous and systematic way to explain the world while keeping the inner child in me alive.
2. Who knew so much thought went into everyday things like a pen? This really nudged me to be more mindful and appreciative of the little wonders around me.
3. I’ve been guilty of confusing familiarity with real, sticky knowledge. For anyone wanting to truly learn for life, this book offers fantastic strategies like retrieval practice (which I’ve been using to post about books!), proper spacing of learning sessions, and mixing related ideas. These methods make the learning curve steeper, but they really help solidify knowledge.
4. The power of our inner voice on our mood and productivity is immense, affecting who we become. If you struggle with negative self-talk, this book has great strategies to minimize its effects. Personal strategies for me include referring to myself in the second or third person, distancing myself by imagining how I’d view the problem in a few years or advising a friend, developing a support system that can provide either of intellectual or emotional support (both are important to have, and not necessarily from the same individuals), spending quiet time in nature, and developing rituals like a short breathing exercise before deep work.
5. Our existence is based on chance, and the next moment isn’t guaranteed. This book teaches important life lessons on focusing on what truly matters and investing in your future while living in the present.
6. Anyone interested in investing should check out this book. It’s packed with great insights on investing in the stock market, explaining why picking individual stocks isn’t as profitable in the long term, understanding tax implications, and figuring out what kind of investor you want to be. Plus, it’s a great time to be an investor with all the tools available today.  #bookrecommendations

A well-written book, had me hooked instantly and all the way through. With engaging stories that clearly articulate the author’s points, it’s perfect for both seasoned investors and casual savers. I picked up some valuable lessons myself… the role of luck, consistency, nonlinearity of compounding, tailed events, and emotions in financial decisions #bookrecommendations
A heartfelt reminder to never abandon the pursuit of my North Star! 💫 #inspiring #bookrecommendations

If your are into triathlon, this is an interesting/insightful book from the guy who completed 5 Ironmans in a week in Hawaii. Also, found his argument on overconsumption of protein by athletes very appealing! #bookrecommendations📚

Great book to reignite the entrepreneurial fire within ❤️‍🔥 I also appreciate shedding light to the important roles women played and the acknowledgment on their underrepresentation by the mainstream media during PayPal mafia era. #bookrecommendations

I vividly remember the time that google street view was introduced and I was goofing around with it trying to understand why such a feature would be useful, my conclusion was that I could use it before going somewhere new so I wouldn’t feel so lost upon arrival. Little I knew that those data were being used as ground truth landmarks to train AI engines of google’s autonomous car project! 😅 This is a great book that explains how transportation has changed civilization over the past century, it’s current limitations, as well as benefits and possible transformations in near future once truly autonomous and driverless cars are mass produced. Thought provoking, insightful, & inspiring! #bookrecommendations

Great discussions with AI pioneers and visionaries… AI will be integrated in every aspect of human life and we live in a time that the building blocks of such life is being developed… truly inspiring conversations that broadens one’s perspective on the field and the endless possibilities within it. I’d recommend hard copy over the audio version thou if you don’t have a strong preference between the two modes #bookrecommendations
Very thorough analysis on impacts of automation (technology revolution) on economy since it’s inception, highly recommend it #bookrecommendations

The first half is basic introduction, second half includes nice perspective on AI ethics and conscientiousness, only recommend it to those completely new to the field and want to understand it holistically #bookrecommendations

I almost never interrupt strangers that seem to be busy minding their own business. Yet, I couldn’t resist the urge to ask a guy on transit that was so passionately reading the book what it was about. What I heard was satisfying enough to mentally add the book to my to read list. It’s shocking how despite of living in a world ran by money, we are not taught how to manage it and become financially secure. Very engaging read that changed some of my core beliefs regarding investing and achieving financial security. #bookrecommendations

I really had underestimated the importance of good night sleep and effect of various drugs on its quality. It’s a great evidence-based and eye opening work, and I highly recommend it to anyone struggling with their sleep patterns #bookrecommendations
Great book on how human greed & clash of cultures shaped global manufacturing process…from a unique insider view #bookrecommendations

Great book: perfect balance of engineering, science, history & business (my ultimate obsession in themes) about how microchips came to be and got so deeply integrated into our lives such that we can no longer imagine a world without them! #bookrecommendations

If going through the rabbit hole of open software forum threads is your thing, this book is for you! 😂 It’s basically that for infancy of Bitcoin, BUT Go for hard copy, audio version of someone reading a thread with subject line and heading of every reply may be maddening! 😅 #bookrecommendations PS kudos to all the pioneers, developers, and early adopters out there that get involved in building a path in the unknown land just for the sake of curiosity, generating knowledge, and pushing some boundaries…

First time giving spiritually themed books a shot and I really didn’t expect to love it. It did a great job explaining and structuring core concepts in relation to one another, sth I really lacked before. Also, provides clear steps to get one started on their journey, details what one might encounter along the way, and how to deal with it. Either don’t read it in one go or don’t expect to make much sense of the second half as some progress on the first section is needed, in my opinion. #bookrecommendations

Really enjoyed this book, deeper dive into smart contacts, codifying law, regulations around blockchain technology, and implications.  #blockchaintechnology is clearly my new obsession 😅, message me any interesting content about the topic plz & thank u 🙏 ☺️ #bookrecommendations

Great book that explains the fundamentals very well, also does a nice job of explaining history of money and technical aspects of blockchain technology, and finally pretty fun read! #bookrecommendations

Overcoming current challenges of blockchain such as scalability and efficiency will make this technology a great alternative for the existing centralized digital business models, with better privacy and utility for its end users, truly an exciting time… The book is a bit too visionary and lacks technical details for my taste (understandably so), yet a great start for learning about the core concepts of blockchain. #bookrecommendations

There has been an ever growing shift from tangle to intangible assets in the global economy, yet not much has changed in terms of laws and regulations governing corporations. This books discusses the implications and potential steps forward that can benefit the society as a whole. Insightful, eye opening, and rich in content, don’t give up on the slow start ;) #bookrecommendations

This book is about the evolution of value theory, it starts off a bit dry but soon gets the blood boiling as it  outlines how the economy has shifted from value creation to value extraction, how the current system may result in hindering of innovation, other implications, and what we can do as a society to address the existing problems #bookrecommendations

Was hooked cover to cover, it’s a biased view of a man on human evolution (as expected), nevertheless rich in content, and I admire the author’s boldness on some of the topics such as religion, moral values, gender, a rarity in today’s world where we all tend to shy away. He definitely pushed me out of my comfort zone by asking the hard questions, and I agree with his claim that with ever faster pace of technological advances we need to start addressing the social, ethical, environmental, and other implications that have risen, so we can fully and responsibly enjoy the benefits. #bookrecommendations

Entertaining and insightful, don’t let the cover deceive you, the main message is not caring less in your life, it’s giving your everything to a meaningful cause that you are willing to suffer for, as for how?! Just do it. He perfectly delineates in the book that there is no how, there is simply action>motivation>inspiration! Thank you @fadymshoukry #bookrecommendations

Great book to learn about micro and macro level economics that every person needs to know in order to make important personal financial decisions. Thanks to my second year econ prof #bookrecommendations

A great book to better understand the disease, patient’s point of view, their caregivers’, the current gaps in the care of patients, the critical decisions that need to be made in timely manner, the challenges faced during prognostic trajectories, and how we could go about preserving the dignity of the patient despite everything... As someone who does research in this field, I also found it eye-opening and inspiring. Thank you @sayehbayat #bookrecommendations
Great book to remind ourselves to not get lost in the tangents of life, the meaning of it all, and one’s purpose. Thank you @fadymshoukry  for #bookrecommendations

Great book about the history of internet, how it evolved to its current form, who owns it, who really benefits from it, and why it is important for us to take action as a whole to improve the system rather than blame “The Faces” for its shortcomings. #bookrecommendations
“I’ve been very dumb when I had no skin in the game... and the most brilliant version of me has come out when I was in it fighting”. A good reminder! #bookrecommendations

We favour the visible, the imbedded, the personal, the narrated, and the tangible. We scorn the abstract. Everything good, aesthetics, ethics, and wrong, fooled by randomness, with us seems to flow from it. The ideas are not path dependent, and the winners have realized to take advantage of randomness by starting every day as a clean slate! We should also realize that optimizing can be miserable and learn the joy of satisficing instead. The only article lady Fortuna has no control over is our behaviour, so what we need is to know how to live in all circumstances... with personal elegance! #alwaysfuntoreadhisbooks #bookrecommendations

A book full of practical tips and great advices for navigating your own definition of success! e.g. opportunity cost is the life you give as the price of anything, let this sink a little! 😉 #bookrecommendations

Some personal takeaways: The timing of this book in my life together with its content resulted in yet another paradigm shift in my way of thinking. The personal meaning of success (dreaming and doing sth about it) is the true alignment between the right role and the right skills, which requires truly knowing oneself (unique intensifiers, the right pond to swim at, & one’s eulogy), while having flexible optimism, being a giver (not a sucker), chooser not a picker (responsive not reactive), practicing grit and quit (at the same time), 80-20% rule on fail-win, self compassion, and showing gratitude toward those who were there along the way. Happiness on the other hand seems to NOT be the result of success, but the cause of it; and it’s sourced from the meaningful relationships in our lives (I had it all wrong!). And Life is more than one stick measure, try multi-dimensional approach using Happiness, Achievements, Significance, and Legacy, in which quality and quantity both matter!

Second time listening to Better by Atul Gawande and still found it amazing. This book helped me transform from being a chronic perfectionist (aka swinging from two poles of procrastination along with justification of circumstances not being ideal to obsessively overworking on every little minor details that were absolute waste of time) to becoming a striver. Striving for perfection, knowing  it is not going to be achieve it, yet not settling for an average, can provide one with the diligence required to achieve greatness, no matter the circumstances or the odds. PS his book “being mortal” is a must read and was absolutely a paradigm shift for me! #bookrecommendations

My timing for listening to this book could have not been any better! I admire Nassim Taleb for two main reasons: his absolute and bold honesty and his elegant framing of concepts from hard to grasp rigour to simple intuitive common sense! Antifragility, meaning of being alive, pitfalls of academia, convexity and Jensen’s inequality (personally I was really struggling with these concepts till I hit the ‘Aha’ moment from his explication hence the perfect timing :P although too bad the relevant chapter came up right after my pset submission deadline...), phonies, how not to be a sucker, and much much more!!! #bookrecommendations

From vacuum tubes, to ladies who pioneered the field of computer programming, semiconductors, transistors, microprocessors, internet, PC, search engine, and AI, this is a great and thorough overview of digital revolution. I also recommend Isaacson‘s book about Steve Jobs. Thanks to my PI, Dr. Cari Whyne, for her amazing graduation gift! #bookrecommendations

It truly takes a creative, playful, and a bright mind to make Stat a fun subject! If you wanna know more about why you should have a diversified investment portfolio, why successful career in academia is more like winning a lottery, and why relying on bell curves can destroy you, put this on your reading list! #bookrecommendations #blackswan

“Think for yourself to decide 1) what you want, 2) what is true, and 3) what you should do to achieve [1] in light of [2], and do so with humility and open-mindedness so that you consider the best thinking available to you.” There is so wisdom in every page for those who want to become effective decisions makers and develop their own life principles. #bookrecommendations #raydalioprinciples

Interesting book full of specific practical advices and fun exercises. One point that left me in awe was realizing how terrible we can be by silencing negative emotions both in ourselves and others, often out of love for the person, But really causing more harm than good in the long run. Pain is one of the greatest teachers in life, yet we are mostly trained to forget or ignore it rather than live it. This is a great book to learn how to become an empathetic listener, I specially recommend it if you have/interact with children! #bookrecommendations #howtolisten

I used to think I was an open minded person until I read this great book and realized how my fixed believes have been holding me back in life. It’s amazing how much you learn when you don’t care about making a fool of yourself! Highly recommend it! #mindset #bookrecommendations
